//: Open Design WebUI configuration example. The first `start` auto-generates a clean webui.config.json from this file, stripping every key whose name begins with // (these are documentation only). You may also copy this file to webui.config.json manually — the // keys are ignored and never affect parsing. Precedence: CLI flags > webui.config.json > environment > defaults.
// port: Browser-facing web port (the web listener). Default 7456.
// daemonPort: Internal daemon port. Default 7457; set to 0 to use a random loopback port (internal, local-only).
// host: Bind address. 127.0.0.1 = local only; 0.0.0.0 = listen on all interfaces for LAN/remote access (set a token in that case).
// token: Access token for direct daemon /api calls. When host is non-loopback and this is empty, one is generated and written back to this file and reused on restart. Browsers reaching /api through web do not need it (it is reverse-proxied).
// openBrowser: Whether to open the browser automatically after start. Default true.
// namespace: Optional. Runtime namespace that determines the IPC socket start/stop/status use to talk to each other. Set a distinct value per instance to isolate multiple instances on one machine; omit = default. Keep start/stop/status pointed at the same config once set.
// dataDir: Optional. Absolute path of the data directory (equivalent to the OD_DATA_DIR env var): database / projects / artifacts / logs. Omit = use the system default location (OD_DATA_DIR or ~/.local/share/open-design).
// lang: Optional. Startup output and UI language, e.g. "en" or "zh-CN". Omit = infer from the system environment (LC_ALL/LANG, etc.).
